code.description: Invite code from the invite email / link.

sources.description: Per-source diagnostics so the UI / customer can see which sources contributed to this page and which are stubbed pending the cross-service fanout.

description.description: Optional description (e.g. which service uses the key).

name.description: Human-readable PAK name (shown in the keys list).

policy_ids.description: IDs of managed policies (workspace_policy rows) to bind to this PAK. Bind one or more — the PAK's effective policy is the union of all bound statements.

display_name.description: Display name shown in console and on receipts.

slug.description: URL-friendly slug. Lowercase alphanumerics, separated by hyphens. Used in URLs and as a stable handle.

email.description: Optional invitee email. When set, the invite is locked to that address.

expires_in_hours.description: Hours until the invite expires. Defaults to 168 (7 days).

max_uses.description: Maximum number of accepts. Defaults to 1. Use higher values for "team link" invites.

role_id.description: UUID of the role to grant on accept. Use to target custom roles.

description.description: Optional human description. Pass `null` to clear an existing description on PATCH.
description.example: Read-only access to all Envoi resources

name.description: Policy name (1-64 chars, alphanumeric + space/underscore/hyphen). Unique per workspace.

policy.description: IAM-style policy. Empty array means "no permissions" — useful as a placeholder before granting any actions.
IAM-style policy granting the role its permissions. Same wire shape as PAK policies. Omit or pass `[]` for a no-permissions role.

policy_id.description: Optional managed-policy id to bind the role to. When set, the role's effective policy comes from the bound managed policy and `policy` (inline) must be omitted.

settings.description: Optional per-service settings blob captured at enable time.

source.description: Which audit table this row came from.

source_ref.description: Stable per-source identifier. Used by the cursor to resume from the same point on the next page.

email_verified_at.description: Timestamp when this account verified its primary email, or null if unverified.

policy.description: Merged effective policy across every managed policy bound to this PAK.

role.description: Caller’s role name in this workspace.

id.description: Canonical workspace UUID. Only present for members — non-members get a uniform low-information response.

policy.description: Effective IAM-style policy for this caller in this workspace.

kid.description: Key ID for matching the kid header on a JWT.

use.description: Key usage. Always `sig` for this endpoint.

keys.description: Array of public JWKs. Token verifiers iterate these by `kid` to find the matching signer.

binding_count.description: Counts of currently-bound consumers — surface this in delete-confirm UX so the operator sees cascade impact.

created_by.description: Account that authored the row. Only revealed to callers with `workspace.audit.read`; plain members see null.

policy.description: IAM-style policy statements bound to this managed policy.

created_by.description: Account that authored the row. Only revealed to callers with `workspace.audit.read`; plain members see null.

policy.description: IAM-style policy statements bound to this managed policy.

token.description: The full plaintext PAK token. Returned ONCE — store it now; it cannot be retrieved later.

pak.description: Number of platform API keys bound to this policy.

role.description: Number of workspace roles bound to this policy.

actions.description: Action strings the statement applies to. Wildcards (e.g. `agora.*`, `*.read`, `*`) supported per `actionMatches` semantics.

effect.description: Whether the statement allows or denies the listed actions on the listed resources.

resources.description: Resource URNs the statement applies to. Use `["*"]` for workspace-wide. Per-resource scoping (e.g. `pcft:agora:community/<uuid>`) is supported by the PAK lane today; the workspace-role authoring API restricts this to `["*"]` for now (see `tasks/platform-auth-api/001`).

policies.description: Managed policies currently bound to this PAK.

token_prefix.description: The first ~14 chars of the token (prefix). Plaintext token is never returned after mint.

email.description: Email address to send reset token

last_used_at.description: Last time this session was used (ISO 8601) or null

user_agent.example: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 14_5_0) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/125.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

policy_ids.description: Replacement set of managed-policy IDs. Empty array is rejected — revoke the PAK to disable it.

session_duration.description: Session duration: "short" (24h), "long" (90d), or integer seconds (3600–7776000). Defaults to 30 days.
Session duration: "short" (24h), "long" (90d), or integer seconds (3600–7776000). Defaults to 30 days.

username.description: Unique username (letters, numbers, dot, underscore, hyphen)

role.description: System role shortcut: 'owner' | 'admin' | 'member'. Either `roleId` or `role` must be supplied.

role_id.description: UUID of the role to assign. Use this to target custom roles.

slug.description: New URL-friendly slug. Same constraints as the create form. Renames are broadcast on the workspace events queue so denormalised consumers (rally-api, future product surfaces) can sync. Old URLs continue to resolve for one rename cycle.

description.description: Optional human description. Pass `null` to clear an existing description on PATCH.
description.example: Read-only access to all Envoi resources

name.description: Policy name (1-64 chars, alphanumeric + space/underscore/hyphen). Unique per workspace.

policy.description: IAM-style policy. Empty array means "no permissions" — useful as a placeholder before granting any actions.
Replaces the role policy if provided. Omit to leave policy unchanged.

policy_id.description: Bind / unbind the role to a managed policy. UUID → bind, null → unbind, omit → leave unchanged.

code.description: Verification code from the email link.

actions.description: Every known workspace action across all enabled service catalogs.

actor_id.description: Account UUID of the actor, or null for system actions.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata attached to the audit row (jsonb).

code.description: Single-use invite code embedded in the invite URL.
Single-use invite code embedded in the invite URL.

member_role.description: Caller’s role name in this workspace.

is_system.description: True for built-in system roles (owner/admin/member); false for custom roles.

name.description: Role name. System role names are `owner`/`admin`/`member`.

policy.description: Effective IAM-style policy. When `policy_id` is set this is the bound managed policy; otherwise it is the inline policy column.

policy_id.description: Managed policy ID if the role is bound to one, else null.

info.description: PlatformUser authentication and workspace administration for ProductCraft. Owns sign-in, sign-up, password reset, workspaces, members, invites, workspace roles, workspace-service toggles, and the introspect endpoint every other backend uses to resolve permissions.
info.title: Platform Auth API

get.summary: Public JWKS for verifying platform-issued JWTs (issuer `https://api.auth.productcraft.co`).

403.description: The session lacks the required action for self-account deletion (defence-in-depth — the route is guarded but the cookie may be present without action access).

404.description: No account row exists for the session principal. Should never fire for a valid session — surfaced for SDKs.

delete.summary: Delete the calling account. Auto-promotes the oldest admin (or oldest non-self member) to owner in any workspace where the departing account is the sole owner.

post.summary: Revoke the server-side session tied to the caller’s current access token

201.description: Reset request accepted. If the email matches an account, an email has been queued.

post.summary: Request a password reset email. The response shape never reveals whether the email matched an account.

201.description: Password reset; all active sessions revoked.

post.summary: Reset password using the email-delivered reset token.
Refresh access token using refresh token
Sign in with email/username and password
Create a new account and sign in

get.summary: Introspect current user with workspace list

post.summary: Resolve a Platform API Key (PAK) to its workspace + IAM-style policy. Downstream services use this with a 60s LRU cache and evaluate the policy via @repo/authz-nestjs evaluatePolicy().

get.summary: Introspect caller's membership + effective policy + enabled services in a workspace
List workspaces the current user belongs to

post.summary: Accept a workspace invite by code

409.description: Slug already taken by another workspace.

patch.summary: Update workspace display name, slug, or settings. Slug renames broadcast a `workspace.renamed` event so denormalised consumers (rally-api) sync.

get.summary: List PAKs in this workspace. Plaintext tokens are NOT returned — only prefixes + metadata.

post.summary: Mint a new PAK. The plaintext `token` is returned ONCE — store it now. The minter can grant at most their own effective permissions.

parameters.description: When `true`, publish a `pak:revoked` Redis fan-out so subscribed services evict their caches immediately instead of waiting out the 60s TTL.
parameters.name: force

delete.summary: Revoke a PAK. Default behaviour: introspect returns null on the next upstream call; downstream services fail-closed within the 60s cache TTL window. Pass `?force=true` to additionally publish a Redis fan-out that evicts the matching cache entry across every PAK-consuming service immediately — use it when rotating a leaked PAK and the 60s window matters.

get.summary: Get a single PAK by id.

patch.summary: Update a PAK’s name or description (does not touch bindings).

400.description: One of: bound > 25 policies; merged > 200 statements; one of the policy ids does not exist or lives in a different workspace.

403.description: Caller cannot grant one or more actions in the merged statement set.

404.description: PAK not found in this workspace.

409.description: Empty bindings — revoke the PAK instead of leaving it unbound.

patch.summary: Replace the managed-policy bindings on a PAK. Re-runs caller-narrowing on the merged union of statements.

get.description: Merged audit timeline across the workspace surfaces. v1 covers `workspace_audit` (this service); per-app Heimdall and per-community Agora rows arrive in a follow-up. Response shape is stable from v1 — clients can build against it today.

parameters.description: ISO-8601 lower bound on `timestamp`. Items older than `since` are not returned.
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `pagination.next_cursor`. Resumes the feed at the same merge boundary.
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: Comma-separated subset of sources to include (e.g. `workspace_audit,heimdall_audit`). Omitted = all enabled sources.
parameters.name: sources

400.description: Invalid query params — `since` not parseable as ISO-8601, or `sources` references an unknown source name. Unrecognized sources are silently dropped; an empty result set after that filter still returns 200.

403.description: The caller is signed in but lacks `workspace.audit.read` for this workspace.

404.description: The `:workspaceSlug` path param does not resolve to a workspace the caller can see.

delete.summary: Remove a member from a workspace (or self-leave)

patch.summary: Update a member's role. Body: { roleId } or { role: "owner"|"admin"|"member" }.

get.summary: List every managed policy in a workspace
List every known workspace action (catalog of statement actions).

404.description: Policy not found in this workspace.

delete.summary: Delete a managed policy. Cascades: API keys lose the binding (deny-all if no other bindings remain); roles fall back to their inline policy column.

200.description: Policy detail. `binding_count.{pak,role}` reports how many things are currently bound — surface this in delete-confirm UX.

404.description: Policy not found in this workspace.

get.summary: Get a managed policy by id

400.description: Invalid policy shape, name, or condition block.

403.description: Caller cannot grant one or more requested actions.

404.description: Policy not found in this workspace.

get.summary: List all roles in a workspace

delete.summary: Delete a custom role (fails if it has members)

patch.summary: Update a role or replace its permission set

get.summary: List services activated on this workspace

204.description: Service disabled. Settings preserved for re-enable.

post.summary: Enable (or re-enable) a service for this workspace

requestBody.description: Replaces the per-service settings jsonb entirely. The body is the settings object itself (no wrapper). Service-specific shape — e.g. envoi tracks default sender, agora tracks ranking weights.

patch.summary: Replace the settings jsonb for an active service
